Package com.enovka.gemini4j.domain

Class Model

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.enovka.gemini4j.domain.Model
  • public class Model
extends Object
    Information about a Generative Language Model.
    Author:
    Everson Novka <enovka@gmail.com>

    • Constructor Detail

      • Model

        public Model​(String name,
             String baseModelId,
             String version,
             String displayName,

      • Model

        public Model()

    • Method Detail

      • builder

        public static Model.ModelBuilder builder()

      • toBuilder

        public Model.ModelBuilder toBuilder()

      • getName

        public String getName()
        Required. The resource name of the Model.

        Format: `models/{model}` with a `{model}` naming convention of:

        `"{baseModelId}-{version}"`

        Examples:

        - `models/chat-bison-001`

      • getBaseModelId

        public String getBaseModelId()
        Required. The name of the base model, pass this to the generation request.

        Examples:

        - `chat-bison`

      • getVersion

        public String getVersion()
        Required. The version number of the model.

        This represents the major version

      • getDisplayName

        public String getDisplayName()
        The human-readable name of the model. E.g. "Chat Bison".

        The name can be up to 128 characters long and can consist of any UTF-8 characters.

      • getDescription

        public String getDescription()
        A short description of the model.

      • getInputTokenLimit

        public Integer getInputTokenLimit()
        Maximum number of input tokens allowed for this model.

      • getOutputTokenLimit

        public Integer getOutputTokenLimit()
        Maximum number of output tokens available for this model.

      • getSupportedGenerationMethods

        public List<String> getSupportedGenerationMethods()
        The model's supported generation methods.

        The method names are defined as Pascal case strings, such as `generateMessage` which correspond to API methods.

      • getTemperature

        public Double getTemperature()
        Controls the randomness of the output.

        Values can range over [0.0,2.0], inclusive. A higher value will produce responses that are more varied, while a value closer to 0.0 will typically result in less surprising responses from the model. This value specifies default to be used by the backend while making the call to the model.

      • getMaxTemperature

        public Double getMaxTemperature()
        Max value supported by model.

        Values can range over [0.1,2.0]

      • getTopP

        public Double getTopP()
        For Nucleus sampling.

        Nucleus sampling considers the smallest set of tokens whose probability sum is at least topP. This value specifies default to be used by the backend while making the call to the model.

      • getTopK

        public Integer getTopK()
        For Top-k sampling.

        Top-k sampling considers the set of topK most probable tokens. This value specifies default to be used by the backend while making the call to the model. If empty, indicates the model doesn't use top-k sampling, and topK isn't allowed as a generation parameter.

      • setName

        public Model setName​(String name)
        Required. The resource name of the Model.

        Format: `models/{model}` with a `{model}` naming convention of:

        `"{baseModelId}-{version}"`

        Examples:

        - `models/chat-bison-001`

        Returns:
        this.

      • setBaseModelId

        public Model setBaseModelId​(String baseModelId)
        Required. The name of the base model, pass this to the generation request.

        Examples:

        - `chat-bison`

        Returns:
        this.

      • setVersion

        public Model setVersion​(String version)
        Required. The version number of the model.

        This represents the major version

        Returns:
        this.

      • setDisplayName

        public Model setDisplayName​(String displayName)
        The human-readable name of the model. E.g. "Chat Bison".

        The name can be up to 128 characters long and can consist of any UTF-8 characters.

        Returns:
        this.

      • setDescription

        public Model setDescription​(String description)
        A short description of the model.
        Returns:
        this.

      • setInputTokenLimit

        public Model setInputTokenLimit​(Integer inputTokenLimit)
        Maximum number of input tokens allowed for this model.
        Returns:
        this.

      • setOutputTokenLimit

        public Model setOutputTokenLimit​(Integer outputTokenLimit)
        Maximum number of output tokens available for this model.
        Returns:
        this.

      • setSupportedGenerationMethods

        public Model setSupportedGenerationMethods​(List<String> supportedGenerationMethods)
        The model's supported generation methods.

        The method names are defined as Pascal case strings, such as `generateMessage` which correspond to API methods.

        Returns:
        this.

      • setTemperature

        public Model setTemperature​(Double temperature)
        Controls the randomness of the output.

        Values can range over [0.0,2.0], inclusive. A higher value will produce responses that are more varied, while a value closer to 0.0 will typically result in less surprising responses from the model. This value specifies default to be used by the backend while making the call to the model.

        Returns:
        this.

      • setMaxTemperature

        public Model setMaxTemperature​(Double maxTemperature)
        Max value supported by model.

        Values can range over [0.1,2.0]

        Returns:
        this.

      • setTopP

        public Model setTopP​(Double topP)
        For Nucleus sampling.

        Nucleus sampling considers the smallest set of tokens whose probability sum is at least topP. This value specifies default to be used by the backend while making the call to the model.

        Returns:
        this.

      • setTopK

        public Model setTopK​(Integer topK)
        For Top-k sampling.

        Top-k sampling considers the set of topK most probable tokens. This value specifies default to be used by the backend while making the call to the model. If empty, indicates the model doesn't use top-k sampling, and topK isn't allowed as a generation parameter.

        Returns:
        this.
